extraction.one_line_summary: ROGLIČ Primož won mountaintop sprint by a bike length in extreme winds, taking 10 bonus seconds to flip 6-second deficit into 4-second overall lead.
tactical_patterns: MASS Enric attacked despite struggling on descents in wind, demonstrating selective aggression on final climb when sheltered positioning mattered less.
Bora-Hansgrohe led chase most aggressively defending KAMNA's jersey, revealing vulnerability when domestiques exhaust themselves early in wind stages.
ROGLIČ timed sprint perfectly from shadows rather than initiating, exploiting chaos from earlier attacks by MASS, CHECCONI, LANDER.
Jumbo-Visma committed GELDERMAN and VAN AERT resources early, suggesting team willing to burn multiple cards when bonus seconds at stake.

rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: won sprint by bike length after sitting in shadows, demonstrating superior positioning discipline and late acceleration in crosswinds
rider_signals.MASS Enric: positive: attacked on final climb despite documented descent struggles earlier, showing selective tactical aggression when terrain suited
rider_signals.CARUSO Damiano: neutral: bridged 23-second gap solo but presence questioned whether working for POTRAGO/LANDER or opportunistic
rider_signals.KAMNA Lennard: negative: required sustained Bora-Hansgrohe domestique support to defend 6-second lead, ultimately lost jersey despite team sacrifice
rider_signals.SIMMONS Quinn: negative: pulled from breakaway while six others continued, suggesting physical or tactical miscalculation
rider_signals.PIDCOCK Thomas: negative: receiving team support from Ineos after previous day crash, indicating lingering effects or positioning deficit
